title: OpenWRT — Network
description: WAN throughput and quality, WiFi AP traffic and client counts, Tailscale VPN, router storage/service health, LAN interfaces, DNS/DHCP stats.

panels.title: WAN Download (RX)
panels.description: Bytes per second received on the WAN interface (internet download)

panels.title: WAN Upload (TX)
panels.description: Bytes per second transmitted on the WAN interface (internet upload)

panels.title: WAN Packets/sec
panels.description: Packets per second on WAN. High PPS with low bytes = many small packets (DNS, keepalives).

panels.title: WAN Errors & Drops
panels.description: Non-zero errors indicate hardware problems, driver issues, or line quality issues.

panels.title: WAN Probe Latency & Jitter
panels.description: Latency and jitter from router-side probes to the gateway, upstream resolver, and public internet. Backed by the textfile WAN-quality script.

panels.title: WAN Probe Packet Loss
panels.description: Packet loss from the same router-side probes. Useful to separate local gateway issues from upstream internet loss.

panels.title: WiFi AP Throughput — Both Bands
panels.description: Traffic on the 2.4 GHz (phy0-ap0) and 5 GHz (phy1-ap0) WiFi access point interfaces

panels.title: WiFi Stations Collector
panels.description: Number of WiFi AP interfaces reporting per-client station data. 0 = the prometheus-node-exporter-lua-wifi_stations package is not installed or not running.

panels.title: WiFi Clients by AP
panels.description: Associated WiFi client count per AP interface. Requires the wifi_stations collector.

panels.title: WiFi Signal by AP
panels.description: Reported signal level per WiFi AP interface. Requires the wifi collector.

panels.title: WiFi AP Quality
panels.description: WiFi quality score reported by iwinfo for each access point interface.

panels.title: WiFi AP Link Bitrate
panels.description: Configured WiFi AP bitrate per radio. Useful for spotting unexpected band or mode changes.

panels.title: All Interface Throughput (RX)
panels.description: RX throughput per interface. Excludes loopback and unused LAN ports (lan2, lan3).

panels.title: All Interface Throughput (TX)
panels.description: TX throughput per interface. Excludes loopback and unused LAN ports.

panels.title: Tailscale VPN Throughput
panels.description: Traffic flowing through the Tailscale VPN tunnel

panels.title: NAT Conntrack Sessions
panels.description: NAT connection tracking table usage vs limit. Approaching the limit causes new connections to fail.

panels.title: DNS Queries
panels.description: DNS query rates. 'Local' = served from cache. 'Forwarded' = sent upstream. 'Unanswered' = failures.

panels.title: DHCP Events
panels.description: DHCP handshake events. Many 'Discover' events indicate devices frequently reconnecting.

panels.title: Filesystem Used %
panels.description: Router storage pressure on persistent overlay storage and tmpfs. High overlay usage often breaks upgrades and package installs.

panels.title: Service Status
panels.description: Current status of key router daemons collected by the textfile service-health script.

panels.title: Filesystem Inode Used %
panels.description: Inode pressure on persistent and tmp filesystems. Inode exhaustion can break package operations even with free bytes left.

panels.title: DHCP Pool Utilization
panels.description: DHCP lease pressure trend from helper script metrics.

panels.title: DHCP Pool Size by Interface
panels.description: Configured DHCP pool size per interface from UCI. Each bar shows how many IP addresses are in that interface's DHCP range.

panels.title: DHCP Lease Lifetime Remaining
panels.description: Minimum and maximum remaining lease lifetime across all active DHCP leases. Min approaching zero means a client is about to renew or lose its address.

panels.title: Helper Metric Freshness
panels.description: Age in seconds of each textfile metric. Larger values mean the matching helper script has stopped updating.

panels.title: Service Enablement
panels.description: Whether each monitored init service is enabled at boot. A running service with Enabled=0 is typically started indirectly or manually.

panels.title: Network Interface Status
panels.description: Current state of all network interfaces

panels.title: IPv6, Link, and Kernel Path Health
IPv6 WAN Health
panels.description: IPv6 control-plane health from helper scripts: WAN6 state, default route, and global address count.

panels.title: IPv6 Prefix Lifetime
panels.description: Remaining delegated prefix validity from WAN6 status. Flat-zero values usually indicate no active delegation.

panels.title: Interface Link Up/Down
panels.description: Kernel carrier/operstate-derived interface link status.

panels.title: Interface Link Speed
panels.description: Current link speed per interface where exposed by the kernel.

panels.title: Softnet Drops by CPU
panels.description: Dropped packets in the kernel softnet path by CPU. Sustained growth indicates packet-processing saturation.

panels.title: Softnet Budget Exhaustion
panels.description: How often softnet processing hit budget limits per CPU.

panels.title: Softnet Packets Processed by CPU
panels.description: Total packets processed by the kernel softnet path per CPU. Compare with the drops panel above to understand what fraction of processed packets are being dropped.

panels.title: TCP Listen Queue Drops
panels.description: TCP connection queue drops and overflows from /proc/net/netstat. Non-zero values indicate the router is under heavy connection load or a SYN flood.

panels.title: Firewall Drops by Chain
panels.description: DROP/REJECT packet rates by chain and IP family from firewall counters.

panels.title: Firewall Traffic by Chain
panels.description: Byte throughput by firewall chain and IP family.

panels.title: SQM Collector Available
panels.description: 1 when tc/qdisc stats are available, 0 when tc is missing on this router.

panels.title: Qdisc Drops and Overlimits
panels.description: Traffic shaping stress indicators from qdisc counters.

panels.title: Qdisc Backlog
panels.description: Current qdisc queue depth in bytes and packets.

panels.title: Qdisc Throughput (Shaped Traffic)
panels.description: Bytes per second actually leaving each qdisc after shaping. Compare with the WAN interface rate to confirm SQM is actively capping traffic.

panels.title: Qdisc Requeue Rate
panels.description: Packets requeued by the traffic shaper per second. Elevated requeue rate alongside high overlimits is expected SQM behaviour; very high rates can indicate scheduler misconfiguration.

panels.title: WiFi Radio Collector
panels.description: 1 when iwinfo-based radio collection is available, 0 when unavailable.

panels.title: WiFi Channel and Frequency
panels.description: WiFi radio channel and center frequency per AP interface.

panels.title: WiFi Noise, TX Power, and Quality
panels.description: Radio conditions and quality from iwinfo.
